name: ILDisturbSignalGroupUpdateBit
title: ILDisturbSignalGroupUpdateBit
description: This value is used in combination with the disturbanceMode.
tables: Deprecated Function This function is deprecated and has been replaced by ILNodeDisturbSignalGroupUpdateBit
Note This function is not available for all OEM packages — depends on the CANoeIL. This function can be used in a global node outside the node context of the IL.
long ILDisturbSignalGroupUpdateBit(dbMsg aMessage, char aSigGroupName[], long disturbanceMode, long disturbanceCount, long disturbanceValue);
Modifies the update bit of a signal group. Different fault injections are possible.This function influences a simulation node with an assigned CANoe interaction layer
aMessage The symbolic name of message in the database containing the signal group.
aSigGroupName Name of the signal group using an update bit.

disturbanceMode Identifies the disturbance mode: 0 Value The disturbance uses the value in disturbanceValue as counter. 1 Freeze The current counter value is transmitted. 2 Random A random value is transmitted as counter.
The disturbance uses the value in disturbanceValue as counter.
The current counter value is transmitted.
A random value is transmitted as counter.
The disturbance uses the value in disturbanceValue as counter.
The current counter value is transmitted.
A random value is transmitted as counter.
disturbanceCount -1 Infinite. 0 Stops the disturbance, e.g.a infinite disturbance. n Number of disturbances.
Stops the disturbance, e.g.a infinite disturbance.
Stops the disturbance, e.g.a infinite disturbance.
disturbanceValue This value is used in combination with the disturbanceMode.

Example // Sets the Signal Group Update Bit to 0variables { long disturbanceMode = 0; // The disturbance uses the value in disturbanceValue as Update Bit. int disturbanceCount = 100; long disturbanceValue = 0;}on key 'a'{ ILDisturbSignalGroupUpdateBit(Message_A, "SignalGroup_A", disturbanceMode, disturbanceCount, disturbanceValue);}
